message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 10 implementation ready for team review">
Team, Step 10 (Someday/Maybe, US-GTD-6) is committed locally (e4c7b73). Ready for review.

## Implementation Summary

### Domain: `SomedayMaybeItem` (struct, not enum)
- Simple struct with `id: SomedayMaybeId`, `user_id: UserId`, `title: TodoTitle`, `created_at: DateTime<Utc>`
- No state machine — items are created, edited, activated (moved to inbox), or deleted
- `SomedayMaybeId(Uuid)` newtype with the standard `new()`, `from_uuid()`, `as_uuid()` pattern
- 4 unit tests covering ID uniqueness, field access, from_parts reconstruction

### Database: `someday_maybe_items` table
- Migration `20240101000009`: `id`, `user_id` (FK to users), `title`, `created_at`
- Index on `user_id`

### Repository: `someday_maybe_repository.rs`
- `insert_someday_maybe_item(executor, &item)` — `impl PgExecutor` for transaction support
- `find_someday_maybe_items_by_user`, `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id`, `delete_someday_maybe_item`, `count_someday_maybe_items`, `update_someday_maybe_title`
- Also generalized `inbox_repository::insert_inbox_item` from `&PgPool` to `impl PgExecutor<'_>` (needed for the activate transaction)

### Service: `someday_maybe_service.rs`
- `add_someday_maybe_item` — validates title, persists
- `list_someday_maybe_items` — all items for user, ordered by created_at ASC
- `delete_someday_maybe_item` — ownership check, permanent removal
- `update_someday_maybe_title` — ownership check, title validation, persist
- `activate_someday_maybe_item` — transaction: creates InboxItem with same title, deletes SomedayMaybeItem

### Routes: `routes/someday_maybe.rs` (replaces placeholder)
- `GET /someday-maybe` — list page with add form, items, empty state
- `POST /someday-maybe` — add item (redirect / HTMX fragment)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/activate` — move to inbox (redirect / HTMX empty body + announce "Moved to Inbox")
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/delete` — delete item (redirect / HTMX empty body)
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` — inline edit form (HTMX)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` — submit edit (redirect / HTMX fragment)
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}` — single item fragment (for cancel edit)

### Templates
- `someday_maybe.html` — list page with add form, empty state, item loop
- `someday_maybe_item.html` — item with Edit link, Activate button, Delete button
- `someday_maybe_edit.html` — inline edit form with Save button and Cancel link
- All actions have unique accessible `aria-label` including item title

### CSS: BEM `.someday-maybe*` classes in components layer
- Mirrors the waiting-for pattern with simpler structure (no "person" field)
- Uses design tokens throughout (no hardcoded values)

### Tests: 25 new integration tests
- Page loading (4): 200 status, heading, empty state, current page nav
- Add (3): redirect, empty title silently ignored, too-long title 422
- HTMX add (2): fragment + announce, empty title 204
- Activate (3): moves to inbox + redirect, HTMX empty body + announce, nonexistent 404
- Delete (3): redirect, HTMX empty body + announce, nonexistent 404
- Edit (2): redirect, HTMX fragment + announce
- A11y (3): accessible activate, delete, edit labels
- Semantic list (1): `<ul>` with `role="list"`
- User isolation (1): users cannot see each other's items
- Form label (1): visible label "Park an idea"
- HTMX edit form (1): inline form with Save/Cancel
- Single item (1): fragment for cancel edit

### Glossary: Updated with
- `SomedayMaybeItem`, `SomedayMaybeId` types (marked Implemented)
- 4 new actions: add, delete, edit, activate (all marked Implemented)
- 7 error types in Domain Errors table
- 3 error copy entries

### Pipeline: 252 Rust tests (89 unit + 163 integration) + 11 Playwright e2e, all green

Please review and provide your CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me do a thorough review of the Step 10 implementation. I'll start with the domain type and work outward.

content.content:
1→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     2→use sqlx::{PgExecutor, PgPool};
     3→use uuid::Uuid;
     4→
     5→use crate::domain::{SomedayMaybeId, SomedayMaybeItem, TodoTitle, UserId};
     6→
     7→struct SomedayMaybeRecord {
     8→    id: Uuid,
     9→    user_id: Uuid,
    10→    title: String,
    11→    created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    12→}
    13→
    14→impl SomedayMaybeRecord {
    15→    fn into_domain(self) -> SomedayMaybeItem {
    16→        let id = SomedayMaybeId::from_uuid(self.id);
    17→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);
    18→        let title = TodoTitle::parse(self.title).expect("stored title should be valid");
    19→
    20→        SomedayMaybeItem::from_parts(id, user_id, title, self.created_at)
    21→    }
    22→}
    23→
    24→pub async fn insert_someday_maybe_item(
    25→    executor: impl PgExecutor<'_>,
    26→    item: &SomedayMaybeItem,
    27→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    28→    sqlx::query!(
    29→        r#"INSERT INTO someday_maybe_items (id, user_id, title, created_at)
    30→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4 :: timestamptz)"#,
    31→        item.id().as_uuid(),
    32→        item.user_id().as_uuid(),
    33→        item.title().as_str(),
    34→        item.created_at() as &DateTime<Utc>,
    35→    )
    36→    .execute(executor)
    37→    .await?;
    38→    Ok(())
    39→}
    40→
    41→pub async fn find_someday_maybe_items_by_user(
    42→    pool: &PgPool,
    43→    user_id: &UserId,
    44→) -> Result<Vec<SomedayMaybeItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    45→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    46→        SomedayMaybeRecord,
    47→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title,
    48→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    49→           FROM someday_maybe_items
    50→           WHERE user_id = $1
    51→           ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    52→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    53→    )
    54→    .fetch_all(pool)
    55→    .await?;
    56→
    57→    Ok(records
    58→        .into_iter()
    59→        .map(|r: SomedayMaybeRecord| r.into_domain())
    60→        .collect())
    61→}
    62→
    63→pub async fn find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(
    64→    pool: &PgPool,
    65→    item_id: &SomedayMaybeId,
    66→) -> Result<Option<SomedayMaybeItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    67→    let record: Option<SomedayMaybeRecord> = sqlx::query_as!(
    68→        SomedayMaybeRecord,
    69→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title,
    70→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    71→           FROM someday_maybe_items WHERE id = $1"#,
    72→        item_id.as_uuid(),
    73→    )
    74→    .fetch_optional(pool)
    75→    .await?;
    76→
    77→    Ok(record.map(|r| r.into_domain()))
    78→}
    79→
    80→pub async fn delete_someday_maybe_item(
    81→    executor: impl PgExecutor<'_>,
    82→    item_id: &SomedayMaybeId,
    83→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    84→    sqlx::query!(
    85→        r#"DELETE FROM someday_maybe_items WHERE id = $1"#,
    86→        item_id.as_uuid(),
    87→    )
    88→    .execute(executor)
    89→    .await?;
    90→    Ok(())
    91→}
    92→
    93→pub async fn count_someday_maybe_items(
    94→    pool: &PgPool,
    95→    user_id: &UserId,
    96→) -> Result<i64, sqlx::Error> {
    97→    let record = sqlx::query!(
    98→        r#"SELECT COUNT(*) as "count!" FROM someday_maybe_items WHERE user_id = $1"#,
    99→        user_id.as_uuid(),
   100→    )
   101→    .fetch_one(pool)
   102→    .await?;
   103→
   104→    Ok(record.count)
   105→}
   106→
   107→pub async fn update_someday_maybe_title(
   108→    pool: &PgPool,
   109→    item_id: &SomedayMaybeId,
   110→    title: &TodoTitle,
   111→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   112→    sqlx::query!(
   113→        r#"UPDATE someday_maybe_items SET title = $1 WHERE id = $2"#,
   114→        title.as_str(),
   115→        item_id.as_uuid(),
   116→    )
   117→    .execute(pool)
   118→    .await?;
   119→    Ok(())
   120→}
   121→

1→use sqlx::PgPool;
     2→
     3→use crate::domain::{
     4→    InboxItem, SomedayMaybeId, SomedayMaybeItem, TodoTitle, TodoTitleError, UserId,
     5→};
     6→use crate::infrastructure::inbox_repository;
     7→use crate::infrastructure::someday_maybe_repository;
     8→
     9→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
    10→pub enum AddSomedayMaybeError {
    11→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
    12→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
    13→    #[error(transparent)]
    14→    Unexpected(anyhow::Error),
    15→}
    16→
    17→#[tracing::instrument(
    18→    name = "add_someday_maybe_item",
    19→    skip(pool, title),
    20→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    21→)]
    22→pub async fn add_someday_maybe_item(
    23→    pool: &PgPool,
    24→    user_id: UserId,
    25→    title: String,
    26→) -> Result<SomedayMaybeItem, AddSomedayMaybeError> {
    27→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(title)?;
    28→    let item = SomedayMaybeItem::new(user_id, title);
    29→
    30→    someday_maybe_repository::insert_someday_maybe_item(pool, &item)
    31→        .await
    32→        .map_err(|e| {
    33→            AddSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
    34→                "Failed to insert someday/maybe item: {e}"
    35→            ))
    36→        })?;
    37→
    38→    tracing::info!("Someday/maybe item added");
    39→    Ok(item)
    40→}
    41→
    42→#[tracing::instrument(
    43→    name = "list_someday_maybe_items",
    44→    skip(pool),
    45→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    46→)]
    47→pub async fn list_someday_maybe_items(
    48→    pool: &PgPool,
    49→    user_id: &UserId,
    50→) -> Result<Vec<SomedayMaybeItem>, anyhow::Error> {
    51→    let items = someday_maybe_repository::find_someday_maybe_items_by_user(pool, user_id)
    52→        .await
    53→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to fetch someday/maybe items: {e}"))?;
    54→
    55→    Ok(items)
    56→}
    57→
    58→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
    59→pub enum DeleteSomedayMaybeError {
    60→    #[error("Someday/maybe item not found")]
    61→    NotFound,
    62→    #[error("Not authorized to delete this someday/maybe item")]
    63→    Unauthorized,
    64→    #[error(transparent)]
    65→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    66→}
    67→
    68→#[tracing::instrument(
    69→    name = "delete_someday_maybe_item",
    70→    skip(pool),
    71→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    72→)]
    73→pub async fn delete_someday_maybe_item(
    74→    pool: &PgPool,
    75→    item_id: &SomedayMaybeId,
    76→    user_id: &UserId,
    77→) -> Result<(), DeleteSomedayMaybeError> {
    78→    let item = someday_maybe_repository::find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(pool, item_id)
    79→        .await
    80→        .map_err(|e| DeleteSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
    81→        .ok_or(DeleteSomedayMaybeError::NotFound)?;
    82→
    83→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
    84→        return Err(DeleteSomedayMaybeError::Unauthorized);
    85→    }
    86→
    87→    someday_maybe_repository::delete_someday_maybe_item(pool, item_id)
    88→        .await
    89→        .map_err(|e| {
    90→            DeleteSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
    91→                "Failed to delete someday/maybe item: {e}"
    92→            ))
    93→        })?;
    94→
    95→    tracing::info!("Someday/maybe item deleted");
    96→    Ok(())
    97→}
    98→
    99→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   100→pub enum UpdateSomedayMaybeError {
   101→    #[error("Someday/maybe item not found")]
   102→    NotFound,
   103→    #[error("Not authorized to edit this someday/maybe item")]
   104→    Unauthorized,
   105→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
   106→    InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError),
   107→    #[error(transparent)]
   108→    Unexpected(anyhow::Error),
   109→}
   110→
   111→#[tracing::instrument(
   112→    name = "update_someday_maybe_title",
   113→    skip(pool, new_title),
   114→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   115→)]
   116→pub async fn update_someday_maybe_title(
   117→    pool: &PgPool,
   118→    item_id: &SomedayMaybeId,
   119→    user_id: &UserId,
   120→    new_title: String,
   121→) -> Result<(), UpdateSomedayMaybeError> {
   122→    let item = someday_maybe_repository::find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(pool, item_id)
   123→        .await
   124→        .map_err(|e| UpdateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   125→        .ok_or(UpdateSomedayMaybeError::NotFound)?;
   126→
   127→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
   128→        return Err(UpdateSomedayMaybeError::Unauthorized);
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(new_title).map_err(UpdateSomedayMaybeError::InvalidTitle)?;
   132→
   133→    someday_maybe_repository::update_someday_maybe_title(pool, item_id, &title)
   134→        .await
   135→        .map_err(|e| {
   136→            UpdateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   137→                "Failed to update someday/maybe item: {e}"
   138→            ))
   139→        })?;
   140→
   141→    tracing::info!("Someday/maybe item updated");
   142→    Ok(())
   143→}
   144→
   145→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   146→pub enum ActivateSomedayMaybeError {
   147→    #[error("Someday/maybe item not found")]
   148→    NotFound,
   149→    #[error("Not authorized to activate this someday/maybe item")]
   150→    Unauthorized,
   151→    #[error(transparent)]
   152→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   153→}
   154→
   155→#[tracing::instrument(
   156→    name = "activate_someday_maybe_item",
   157→    skip(pool),
   158→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   159→)]
   160→pub async fn activate_someday_maybe_item(
   161→    pool: &PgPool,
   162→    item_id: &SomedayMaybeId,
   163→    user_id: &UserId,
   164→) -> Result<InboxItem, ActivateSomedayMaybeError> {
   165→    let mut tx = pool.begin().await.map_err(|e| {
   166→        ActivateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to begin transaction: {e}"))
   167→    })?;
   168→
   169→    let item = someday_maybe_repository::find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(pool, item_id)
   170→        .await
   171→        .map_err(|e| ActivateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   172→        .ok_or(ActivateSomedayMaybeError::NotFound)?;
   173→
   174→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
   175→        return Err(ActivateSomedayMaybeError::Unauthorized);
   176→    }
   177→
   178→    let inbox_item = InboxItem::new(user_id.clone(), item.title().clone());
   179→
   180→    inbox_repository::insert_inbox_item(&mut *tx, &inbox_item)
   181→        .await
   182→        .map_err(|e| {
   183→            ActivateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   184→                "Failed to insert inbox item: {e}"
   185→            ))
   186→        })?;
   187→
   188→    someday_maybe_repository::delete_someday_maybe_item(&mut *tx, item_id)
   189→        .await
   190→        .map_err(|e| {
   191→            ActivateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   192→                "Failed to delete someday/maybe item: {e}"
   193→            ))
   194→        })?;
   195→
   196→    tx.commit().await.map_err(|e| {
   197→        ActivateSomedayMaybeError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to commit transaction: {e}"))
   198→    })?;
   199→
   200→    tracing::info!("Someday/maybe item activated (moved to inbox)");
   201→    Ok(inbox_item)
   202→}
   203→

content.text: Now let me check the integration tests and the activate transaction more carefully — I noticed something in the service.
Now let me check one thing I spotted in the service layer — the activate function uses `pool` instead of `&mut *tx` for the find query.
I see the issue. Let me verify the existing clarify functions to compare — do they use `&mut *tx` for the find query or `pool`?

Found it. The clarify functions use `&mut *tx` for the find query (line 136), but `activate_someday_maybe_item` uses `pool` directly (line 169). This is a consistency issue and a potential correctness issue — the find should read within the transaction to ensure the item hasn't been concurrently modified/deleted between the read and the write.

Now let me also check the `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id` repository function to see if it accepts `impl PgExecutor` or only `&PgPool`.

    66→) -> Result<Option<SomedayMaybeItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    67→    let record: Option<SomedayMaybeRecord> = sqlx::query_as!(
    68→        SomedayMaybeRecord,
    69→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title,
    70→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    71→           FROM someday_maybe_items WHERE id = $1"#,
    72→        item_id.as_uuid(),
    73→    )
    74→    .fetch_optional(pool)
    75→    .await?;
    76→

    78→}

content.text:
Confirmed — `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id` takes `&PgPool`, not `impl PgExecutor`. That's why the service function passes `pool` instead of `&mut *tx`. This is a Minor issue — the repository function should accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` to support being called within a transaction, matching the pattern used by `find_inbox_item_by_id`.

Let me verify that `find_inbox_item_by_id` does accept `impl PgExecutor`.

input.content:
CONSENSUS: APPROVED (pending Minor fix)

Step 10 (Someday/Maybe) full code review. Thorough and well-executed. Here is my assessment:

**Domain** (`src/domain/someday_maybe_item.rs`):
- Simple struct, exactly the right shape. Mirrors InboxItem perfectly.
- `SomedayMaybeId` derives `PartialEq, Eq` -- lesson from Step 8 applied. Good.
- 4 unit tests: ID uniqueness, UUID roundtrip, field access, from_parts reconstruction. Clean.
- Doc comment on the struct is concise and accurate.

**Repository** (`src/infrastructure/someday_maybe_repository.rs`):
- Clean separation with `SomedayMaybeRecord` and `into_domain()`.
- `insert_someday_maybe_item` and `delete_someday_maybe_item` accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` for transaction support. Good.

**Service** (`src/services/someday_maybe_service.rs`):
- All four error types follow the established pattern. `tracing::instrument` on all functions.
- `add_someday_maybe_item` parses title first, then creates and persists. Correct.
- `delete_someday_maybe_item` and `update_someday_maybe_title` both verify ownership. Correct.
- `activate_someday_maybe_item` uses the transactional pattern correctly — create InboxItem, delete SomedayMaybeItem, commit.

**1 Minor issue (should fix before commit):**
Line 169 in `someday_maybe_service.rs`: `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(pool, item_id)` uses `pool` instead of `&mut *tx`. The established pattern in `clarify_as_next_action` (inbox_service.rs:136) uses `&mut *tx` for the find query — the read should happen within the transaction for consistency and to prevent a TOCTOU race (item could be deleted between the read and the write).

To fix:
1. Change `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id` in the repository to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` instead of `&PgPool` (matching `find_inbox_item_by_id`).
2. Change the activate service call to pass `&mut *tx` instead of `pool`.

**Routes** (`src/routes/someday_maybe.rs`):
- All 7 handlers follow established patterns. Error handling is consistent.
- `build_view` helper is clean — same pattern as WaitingForView.
- `render_someday_maybe_with_error` extracted for reuse by add and edit error paths. Good — avoids duplication.
- HTMX announce messages are clear: "Idea parked", "Moved to Inbox", "Item deleted", "Item updated".
- `get_edit_someday_maybe` and `get_someday_maybe_item` correctly verify ownership before returning content.

**Templates:**
- `someday_maybe.html`: Empty state with encouraging copy, `hidden` attribute on list when empty, add form with visible label "Park an idea". All good.
- `someday_maybe_item.html`: `<time>` element for date (lesson from Step 8 a11y review applied). All three action buttons have `aria-label` with title disambiguation. Good.
- `someday_maybe_edit.html`: Cancel link with `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }}"`. Visually hidden label on input. Good.

**Tests** (`tests/api/someday_maybe.rs`):
- 25 integration tests with excellent coverage. All CRUD paths tested, both HTMX and non-HTMX.
- Activate test verifies both removal from someday/maybe AND appearance in inbox — end-to-end verification. Good.
- User isolation test. Good.
- A11y label tests for all three actions. Good.

**Four rules of simple design:**
1. Passes all tests (252 Rust + 11 e2e) -- yes
2. Reveals intention -- yes, naming is clear throughout
3. No duplication -- `build_view` and `render_someday_maybe_with_error` prevent duplication in routes
4. Fewest elements -- simple struct, no unnecessary abstractions

Fix the Minor (find within transaction) and this is ready to ship.

Step 10 (Someday/Maybe) full code review. Thorough and well-executed. Here is my assessment:

**Domain** (`src/domain/someday_maybe_item.rs`):
- Simple struct, exactly the right shape. Mirrors InboxItem perfectly.
- `SomedayMaybeId` derives `PartialEq, Eq` -- lesson from Step 8 applied. Good.
- 4 unit tests: ID uniqueness, UUID roundtrip, field access, from_parts reconstruction. Clean.
- Doc comment on the struct is concise and accurate.

**Repository** (`src/infrastructure/someday_maybe_repository.rs`):
- Clean separation with `SomedayMaybeRecord` and `into_domain()`.
- `insert_someday_maybe_item` and `delete_someday_maybe_item` accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` for transaction support. Good.

**Service** (`src/services/someday_maybe_service.rs`):
- All four error types follow the established pattern. `tracing::instrument` on all functions.
- `add_someday_maybe_item` parses title first, then creates and persists. Correct.
- `delete_someday_maybe_item` and `update_someday_maybe_title` both verify ownership. Correct.
- `activate_someday_maybe_item` uses the transactional pattern correctly — create InboxItem, delete SomedayMaybeItem, commit.

**1 Minor issue (should fix before commit):**
Line 169 in `someday_maybe_service.rs`: `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(pool, item_id)` uses `pool` instead of `&mut *tx`. The established pattern in `clarify_as_next_action` (inbox_service.rs:136) uses `&mut *tx` for the find query — the read should happen within the transaction for consistency and to prevent a TOCTOU race (item could be deleted between the read and the write).

To fix:
1. Change `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id` in the repository to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` instead of `&PgPool` (matching `find_inbox_item_by_id`).
2. Change the activate service call to pass `&mut *tx` instead of `pool`.

**Routes** (`src/routes/someday_maybe.rs`):
- All 7 handlers follow established patterns. Error handling is consistent.
- `build_view` helper is clean — same pattern as WaitingForView.
- `render_someday_maybe_with_error` extracted for reuse by add and edit error paths. Good — avoids duplication.
- HTMX announce messages are clear: "Idea parked", "Moved to Inbox", "Item deleted", "Item updated".
- `get_edit_someday_maybe` and `get_someday_maybe_item` correctly verify ownership before returning content.

**Templates:**
- `someday_maybe.html`: Empty state with encouraging copy, `hidden` attribute on list when empty, add form with visible label "Park an idea". All good.
- `someday_maybe_item.html`: `<time>` element for date (lesson from Step 8 a11y review applied). All three action buttons have `aria-label` with title disambiguation. Good.
- `someday_maybe_edit.html`: Cancel link with `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }}"`. Visually hidden label on input. Good.

**Tests** (`tests/api/someday_maybe.rs`):
- 25 integration tests with excellent coverage. All CRUD paths tested, both HTMX and non-HTMX.
- Activate test verifies both removal from someday/maybe AND appearance in inbox — end-to-end verification. Good.
- User isolation test. Good.
- A11y label tests for all three actions. Good.

**Four rules of simple design:**
1. Passes all tests (252 Rust + 11 e2e) -- yes
2. Reveals intention -- yes, naming is clear throughout
3. No duplication -- `build_view` and `render_someday_maybe_with_error` prevent duplication in routes
4. Fewest elements -- simple struct, no unnecessary abstractions

Fix the Minor (find within transaction) and this is ready to ship."
  }
}

content.text: Review sent. One Minor issue found: in `activate_someday_maybe_item`, the `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id` call at line 169 uses `pool` instead of `&mut *tx`, which is inconsistent with the established clarify pattern (where all reads happen within the transaction). The repository function also needs to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` to support this. Everything else is clean — 25 well-structured tests, consistent patterns throughout, good a11y practices carried forward from Step 8 lessons.
